entry.title: Re: SAP Business Technology Platform API Management layer.
entry.content:
After looking into this further and weighing the pros and cons, I’ve decided on a path that balances governance with performance. I wanted to share it here in case it helps others.

The Conclusion: APIM by Default We’ve decided to stick with SAP BTP API Management as our primary gateway for both internal and external traffic. While bypassing it for internal apps seems tempting for speed, the 'extra hop' latency is actually negligible (usually just a few milliseconds). The risk of losing central visibility and security control is a much bigger concern than the minor speed gain.

A Simple Example of Why:

  • The Problem: If we let internal apps call S/4HANA directly, we lose the ability to see who is calling what. If an internal app suddenly starts sending too many requests, it could slow down the entire S/4 system for everyone.

  • The Solution: By keeping them in APIM, we can set a 'Quota' policy. This acts like a safety valve—if an app goes over its limit, APIM catches it before it ever hits our core S/4 system.

How we are handling the 'Internet' concern: For our most sensitive internal traffic, we are looking into SAP BTP Private Link. This gives us the best of both worlds: we get the full governance and analytics of API Management, but the data travels over a private connection rather than the public internet.

Final Rule of Thumb: We will use APIM for everything to keep a 'Clean Core.' We will only allow a direct connection as a rare exception for massive data transfers where every millisecond is critical, but those will be documented case-by-case.

entry.title: Display corresponding infotype settings when switching between different countries in PA30.
entry.content:
1、We have a global project with users in three regions: Chinese mainland, Taiwan region of China, and Vietnam.

2、Display country grouping–specific infotype settings when switching among country groupings 99, 42, and 28 in PA30.

3、How to enable a user to switch between country/region-specific infotype displays in PA30 after logging on.

For example, when viewing an employee in Vietnam and selecting the relevant country grouping, the infotype display can be switched to show the infotypes under country grouping 99.

4、I have attempted to configure it using the settings below, but it did not work.

entry.title: Re: Compensation template calc fields
entry.content:
Hi, for the system-calculated fields, the Implementation Guide on the SAP Help portal is a very valuable resource. This is the link to the one for Compensation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-compensation/implementing-and-managing-compensation/standard-columns-in-compensation-plan-template

You can do a CTRL+F or use the search at the top to find the information about the columns you're looking for. There is a similar guide for Variable Pay as well.

entry.title: Re: Stock replication trigger behavior in SAP_COM_0447
entry.content:
Dear @Melis_Sorgun,

Question 1
No. SAP_COM_0447 / Material Stock Replicate is documented as a SOAP outbound replication service. SAP Public Cloud inventory API catalog lists Material Stock Replicate, Material Stock Read and MaterialDocumentCreateNotification_Async_Out as separate artifacts so SAP_COM_0447 should not be positioned as the standard immediate trigger for every GR, GI or transfer posting

Question 2
Use the material document notification pattern rather than the stock replication service itself. In the same Public Cloud inventory API area SAP documents MaterialDocumentCreateNotification_Async_Out separately from stock replication which supports treating the material document as the triggering object when inventory postings occur

Question 3
The recommended Public Cloud design is use material document notification as the trigger, identify the affected material / plant / storage location then call Material Stock Read to retrieve the updated stock quantity and forward that result to the external system. If the landscape requires event distribution or buffering Public Cloud also supports integration with SAP Event Mesh

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/1891d652a8d54a46a587bc11a0a713c7.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6ef7f849fed34f95adfc449f29835255/f5cb5d8e09db4e609d1882a25ec0ee39.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/7d77050a7f084207affb68455b5b7b13.html

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implement-an-integration-of-sap-s-4hana-cloud-with-sap-commerce-cloud/setting-up-the-integration-configuration_cc037ffe-9b4a-47a5-ba7f-6921d4b8c3bb

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to maintain system configuration data in SAP BTP
entry.content:
@patrick_winkler - Good write up here. Stumbled upon this and its been helpful. I was able to test this out by adding some entries to a Z table and then recording them on a customizing transport which seems to work. Regarding the portion here where we pass along the table contents for recording. I created a couple of entries -> passed table content -> new task was created under my customizing transport, and I can see the table object and keys I recorded. 

If I wanted to delete all entries of the z table, would we still keep this the same? I figured we would collect all entries, perform a deletion of table and then pass along the table contents to ADD_TABU_CONTENT like we did when we created entries initially? 

  transport_service->get_transport_recorder( )->add_tabu_content( zstab_content ).

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Technology Platform API Management layer.
entry.content:
Just make sure you define a good naming criteria for APIM. Because, there are no groupings or folder structure available. Also a strategy to defined versions for APIs and how you transport them. Those have been few pain points that I observed. 

Good luck!

entry.title: ZSEMC and MK03
entry.content: I am digging into the relationship between MK03 and ZSEMC.  During some routine po creation, we send pos using ZSEMC, email cockpit, to communicate the PO directly to the vendor.  During this time we found some emails within ZSEMC that were no longer valid.  I am told that the emails in ZSEMC are generated based on the data in MK03 (or variant).  When we check MK03 these emails are not listed as options, and checking the history of changes, they do not show as ever being options within MK03.  I am trying to understand how emails would be added to ZSEMC outside of the MK03 functionality.  I'm also trying to align so i can get these removed somehow, is there a direct connection?  Is there another SAP transaction that marries the data between itself and ZSEMC that could be the storage of these outdated emails we want to remove?  Is there a way within ZSEMC that i can find the historical on when a specific email was added?

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Onboarding
entry.content:
Prerequisites

  1. The BDC license is purchased, and you have received the welcome email. After your organization has purchased SAP Business Data Cloud, the user designated as the contact person receives an invitation email with a link to SAP for Me. If you’re unsure about your registration as a contact person for SAP Business Data Cloud, contact your Sales Account Executive or the Customer Interaction Center (CIC).

    If you are from a third party of the SAP customer, then reach out to your contact on the SAP customer side. They must provide you with this email. Note: Do not open a support Case with SAP Support as they cannot resend or provide you with this email.

  2. You will also require an S-user with Super Admin or Cloud Admin access in SAP for Me. Refer to KBA 2492132 What is the difference between Super, Cloud, and User Administrator S-user IDs? - SAP for Me and more information available here Understanding S-Users and SAP for Me

Outline Steps

  1. Provisioning the BDC Cockpit is the first step and you cannot proceed to provision anything else until this step is completed. We provide step by step instructions in the Community Blog Part 1: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud through SAP4Me
  2. Then provision the BDC Core components of Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), Databricks as per your business requirements. Refer to steps in Provisioning SAP Business Data Cloud Applications

Resources

Before embarking on the provisioning process, it is important to educate yourself on the entire process.

  1. The Business Data Cloud Resource Center is your starting point for this journey



    There are many resources available here including: 
    1. the SAP BDC Onboarding Webcast which should be mandatory viewing for anyone who is about to provision SAP BDC.
    2. full video of the provisioning process here: Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud and its core components
    3. SAP BDC Onboarding – this is the official product documentation which explains in detail the entire provisioning process.
    4. SAP BDC Community Blog series here Introductory Guide to Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud – this has a series of blogs with screenshots showing the step by step process.

  2. If you still have questions or if you would like to have an SAP onboarding expert walk you through the provisioning process, you can book a 1:1 onboarding session with SAP via SAP Business Data Cloud -> “If you still have questions, request a 1:1 onboarding session here”.

  3. If you encounter technical issues during the actual provisioning process, then you may open a Support Case via the SAP for Me portal.
  4. If you have questions outside of SAP Technical Support area of responsibility or if you would like to leverage the knowledge and experience of other SAP BDC Administrators and users, post your questions to the SAP Business Data Cloud - SAP Community Q&A space.

Additional Resources

  • Official Product documentation here: SAP Business Data Cloud
  • SAP Community Blog SAP Business Data Cloud - From Provisioning to Intelligent Applications
  • SAP Community Blog SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
  • SAP Community Blog Rewiring of SAP Datasphere to SAP Business Data Cloud
  • KBA 3733691 SAP BDC Onboarding FAQ
  • KBA 2626344 Who is the SAP Account Manager/Executive for my company?
  • KBA 3705747 Provisioning failure due to duplicate host name during SAP Business Data Cloud Cockpit

entry.title: Re: How I Connected Claude AI to My SAP ABAP System Using MCP — A Complete Windows Guide
entry.content:
muchas gracias por este post y queria aprovechar para realizar la siguiente pregunta. ¿Como se que la conexión a SAP se realiza correctamente y como puedo configurar el .env con SapRouter? 

Gracias y que tengas un feliz dia.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Technology Platform API Management layer.
entry.content:
Thanks, @VijayKonam  That is a very fair point. The lack of folders in APIM can definitely lead to a 'wild west' feel if you aren't careful.

To address those pain points, we are looking at:

  • Strict Naming Convention: We’re planning a prefix-based system (e.g., "INT_" for internal, "EXT_"  for external, followed by the functional area like "BILL" or  " LOG"  ) to make filtering easier since we can't use folders.

  • Version Control: We intend to use the header-based versioning or URL path versioning (like "/V1/") to ensure we don't break existing consumer apps when S/4 services evolve.

  • Automated Transport: We are exploring SAP CI/CD service or Project "Piper" to automate the transport of API proxies. Doing this manually through zip files is definitely a recipe for configuration errors.

I really appreciate the 'heads up' on these—it helps us avoid those traps before we go live!. So far 5 days no defects but we went more we will get to know any real life cases. so far so good although.

entry.title: I reverse‑engineered MM invoice accounting (and here's your new automatic analysis tool!)
entry.content:
I’ve spent the last few years in SAP Support staring at MM invoices that “don’t make sense.”
If you recognize subjects like:

  • “GRIR can not be cleared”

  • “Posting and reversal amount does not match”
  • “Exchange rate difference between GR and IR settling to material account”
  • “Unexpected UMB revaluation posting in MIRO”

…then you already know the kind of stories I’m talking about.

In theory, GR/IR should be simple: goods go in, invoice comes later, the clearing account should end up at zero and reversals should “just mirror” the original. In practice, you add price changes, condition updates, partial goods receipts, provisional/final invoicing, several currencies, and postings across periods – and suddenly the GR/IR line or the reversal document looks like it came from another universe.

For a long time, all we could do would be to share KBAs explaining the standard design and the complex calculations that actually occur in the back-end. This is not efficient, and increases both engineer effort and customer effort.

So I decided to do something slightly extreme: reverse‑engineer the MM invoice accounting logic and encode it into a report that tells the story for you.

The old way

If you’ve ever tried to really understand a tricky MIRO or MR8M document, you’ve probably met some of these notes:

  • 1674290 – Invoice Posting Logic – SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA

  • 1609927 – Credit Memo Posting Logic – SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA
  • 1789924 – Subsequent Debit/Credit Posting Logic – SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA
  • 3382819 – MR11/MR11SHOW: Calculation logic for GR/IR clearing – SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA
  • 3039200 – MIRO: KDM and PRD differences – SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA

Together, they explain the core clearing logic very well: which quantities and values are used, how WRX is derived, what happens when subsequent debits/credits are handled, what's different in MR11, and how KDM/PRD differences are supposed to look.

The problem is not the documentation; the problem is how much work it takes to apply it:

  • You need to dig out all GR and IR documents for the PO item.

  • You need to consider the right currency context.
  • You need to apply the formulas on top of the customer’s specific history.
  • And you need to do it again for the next incident.

This is exactly where the older IV_WRX_ANALYSIS report was already a big step forward: it automated the WRX clearing calculation and showed that the system was simply following its own rules.

But that approach had some important limits:

  • It focused on WRX only (GR/IR account line).

  • It worked mainly in 1st local currency.
  • It concentrated on the final calculation, not on the whole story behind the document.

At some point I realized: for the kind of questions we see today, “WRX only” is not enough.

What consultants actually ask us

Here is how MM invoice issues usually arrive in Support:

  • “GRIR quantity is equal but PO is open due to currency conversion.”

  • “GRs and IRs imbalance at LC3 level while LC1/LC2 are fine.”
  • “Exchange rate difference between GR and IR is settling to material account; we expected KDM.”
  • “MIRO posting triggers UMB account and we want to know why.”
  • “MR8M: Posting and reversal amount does not match”
  • “New 0‑value line in local currency (LC3) in all VIM invoices after an upgrade.”

Notice what’s going on here:

  • Customers rarely say “Please show me the formula from Note 1674290.”

  • They say “Why is this GR/IR line so strange?” or “Why is this reversal not a mirror?”

All these scenarios have one thing in common:
You can answer them with the existing notes and manual calculations – but only if you’re ready to become a part‑time human calculator.

I wanted something better for consultants: a way to get the same level of explanation without having to rebuild all equations every time.

What I decided to build instead

Therefore, it is not enough to show to the consultants how WRX was calculated. It would be much better to access the whole story behind the invoice:

  • Which documents were taken into account.

  • How quantities and values accumulated over time.
  • How the system split the outcome into stock (BSX), price differences (PRD), exchange‑rate differences (KDM), GR/IR clearing (WRX), and sometimes revaluation (UMB) and other lines.
  • And, specially, why the accounting document is following standard design and, even if the amounts look strange, the system knows it did the right thing

So I built a new analysis report with a very simple idea: If you’re going to trust the accounting document, let’s show you exactly what the system saw and how it used it.

Design goals were straightforward:

  1. Show the entire PO/SA history for the invoice item
    Not just a final WRX total, but a line‑by‑line list of relevant GRs and IRs, so it’s obvious which documents the system actually considered.

  2. Support multiple currencies
    Consultants can switch the report to document currency or up to three local currencies, which is crucial when GR/IR appears balanced in one currency but strange in another.
  3. Explain the logic, not just print numbers
    After the history tables, the report has a “brain” section that walks through the main steps of the posting logic – in a way that matches the KBAs, but applied to the specific case you’re analyzing.
  4. Keep the input simple
    On the selection screen you basically tell the report:
    • which invoice item to analyze,
    • which currency type you want to see, and
    • in case of multiple account assignment, which account assignment line.

  5. Make it easy to share and debug
    When a customer sends the report output back to SAP, the structure is friendly both for humans and for any future tooling that needs to understand what happened.

And because the older IV_WRX_ANALYSIS report was already useful, I didn’t throw it away: I used it as a basis for this new solution.

What the new report actually shows

Without going into technical field names, here’s how the output is organized and why it helps.

Part 1: The ingredients

  1. Context block – what are we analyzing?
    At the top you see the invoice number, fiscal year, item, the PO reference and the general context.

  2. Goods Receipt history – “how did we get here on the GR side?”
    The first table lists all relevant GR documents for that PO item, and explains how GR quantities and amounts contribute to the final totals.

  3. Invoice history – “what happened before this invoice?”
    The next table does the same for previous IRs (and subsequent debits/credits), including reversals.
    It shows how much has already been invoiced before the document you’re analyzing.

Part 2: The Recipe

  1. Logic explanation 
    This is the part that replaces pages of manual calculation:

    • It shows how the GR/IR clearing line is derived, so you can see why WRX doesn’t always match the item amount.

    • It explains how the system splits the amount into:

      • Stock postings (BSX),
      • Price differences (PRD),
      • Exchange‑rate differences (KDM),
      • and, when relevant, revaluation (UMB) and other lines such as EIN, EKG, FRE etc.

    You don’t have to know every detail of the formulas from the KBAs – the report is essentially doing those calculations for you and showing the intermediate steps.

Part 3: The results

Final amounts – “what should the FI document look like?”
At the end, the report prints the final calculated amounts per transaction key, in a format that mirrors the accounting document.
This is the part you can compare directly with the FI document. If they match, you’ve just proved that the system is behaving as designed (and you didn’t need a spreadsheet to get there).

Your turn

The report is intentionally not a monster transaction. It’s something you can run quickly whenever a document is questioned and you need to audit results and investigate documents that look wrong.

To keep things simple (and consistent with SAP Support processes), the report ZIV_ACCOUNTING_ANALYSIS is delivered via SAP Note 2427297 – Analysis report to explain IV accounting documents

From there you can:

  • Download the report source from the attachments of the note and implement it manually via SE38 in your system, as described in the note.

    • The report is not available for SNOTE implementation. For that reason, you need to get the attachment for manual implementation
  • Then run it like any other custom analysis tool, using the straightforward selection screen.

The note itself remains the official reference for obtaining and implementing the report; the blog post you’re reading is about why it exists and how it can help you as a consultant.

entry.title: Re: Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
Hi quick question.

 

We have been trying to do an integration with using Ariba Buying and Invoicing with a custom third party tax integration, wlthough we have been able to get the actual integration and proper tax calculations back in the Invoice Reconciliation step The expected tax amount is accuratly reflecting the calculations for our third party service but the amount on invoice is always defaulting to 0.00 even though when I view the invoice lines it does show an amount other than 0.00.  Are there any ideas on how we can fix this.

User - GE307977

entry.title: Observations on NSE Implementation for CDPOS Table in QS1
entry.content:
We would like to share observations from a recently completed project. We implemented an NSE setup for the CDPOS table in QS1, where the table was partitioned into three parts and moved to warm storage. Currently, all three partitions reside in warm storage, and the overall RAM consumption of the CDPOS table is approximately 1.2 GB.

However, we have observed that the RAM consumption of the CDPOS table increases whenever data from the warm partitions is accessed. We would like to understand whether the system is loading data from warm storage into hot storage even when the request is only for read access.

If the system is indeed reloading data into hot storage upon each read request, we would also like to know when this data gets unloaded. Additionally, is there a way to explicitly unload the data from hot storage (buffer cache) immediately after the request has been processed?

entry.title: Re: I reverse‑engineered MM invoice accounting (and here's your new automatic analysis tool!)
entry.content: Great work, Giovani! The report is working very well; it will address a lot of questions from our customers.

entry.title: Re: Set employee personnel number ranges in s/4 HANA Publ...
entry.content:
Dear @ZakariaELMartaoui,

Changing the Personnel Number (PERNR) to a customer-controlled external number is not supported in Public Cloud. SAP states that having the same number range for Business Partner and employee is not supported in Public Cloud due to predefined configuration, and that worker numbering is dynamically controlled by SAP and cannot be modified by end users

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3564479

For this reason the requirement should not be implemented by trying to replace the SAP personnel number with the Workday ID. Instead SAP standard model keeps the externally provided workforce/work-assignment identifier separate from the internally used SAP personnel number. SAP Help documents standard content for Map Work Assignment External ID and Personnel Number in BP Web Service and also a standard object for Mapping Between Work Agreement Internal and External IDs

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/6defce56155241659b82e05694439b78.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/9bf04ed236384d1cad260c5b7782c4cf.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/3a430c1a05ea4a3181be576d943ad438.html

So the SAP-supported approach is to keep the Workday ID as the external ID and let SAP continue to generate and use the internal PERNR. If the business requirement is for Finance users to see the Workday ID on the payment-related UI this should be handled as a display/mapping requirement, not as a personnel-number-range change. That conclusion follows from SAP standard numbering restriction together with the delivered external-to-internal ID mapping model

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Does PRIMARY KEY clustered check stored somewhere
entry.content:
The SYSTAB table column clustered_index_id is NOT NULL and set to the index id for the clustered index.  You can use that value to find the index in SYSIDX andSYSIDXCOL. 

Here an as-is query that returns clustered index details in a database:

select
      st.table_name as TableName
    , si.index_name as IndexName
    , list(
        string(stc.column_name, ' ', 
            case sic.[order] 
                when 'A' then 'asc' 
                when 'D' then 'desc' 
            end case
        ), ', '
      order by st.table_name, si.index_name, sic.sequence) as IndexColumns
from 
    systabcol stc join systab st join sysidx si join sysidxcol sic
where 
        st.clustered_index_id = si.index_id
    and st.table_id = si.table_id
    and stc.column_id = sic.column_id
group by st.table_name,

The IndexColumns column consolidates the cluster index columns in a consolidated way for each index returned rather than a separate row for each column in the index.

entry.title: Re: Identifying and mitigating potential DDoS attacks in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content: I came across this older discussion and wanted to add that newer traffic analytics tools in SAP Commerce Cloud Control Center make spotting suspicious spikes much easier now. Has anyone here tried combining rate‑limit rules with edge caching to reduce the load during bursts? I’d be curious to hear what setups others are using and whether you’ve seen better stability during heavy traffic.

entry.content: Such a nice post 😁 It’s really great to hear your perspective on using Business Builders. Thank you for sharing, Luis!

entry.title: Re: Introducing Our New Reply Progression Badges and a Color Refresh!
entry.content:
Hi @caroleighdeneen,

It would be fun to see color variations on the badges! My favorite color is Teal.

entry.title: Re: No Bell icon notification for Project Billing Request...
entry.content:
Dear @varunjain134,

If the approver already receives the Project Billing Request approval item in Inbox then the workflow itself is working correctly. SAP explicitly states that all scenarios created via Manage Workflow for Project Billing Requests appear in the approvers inbox. SAP general flexible workflow documentation also says that approvers are informed by SAP Fiori launchpad notifications by default with e-mail as an additional notification option

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/3e12d72e4ae74098872b32f7d7518676.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/bd5b25de619d4f4da609fa86261a1126.html

https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226/2308.502/en-US/WN_CE2308.2_EN.pdf

 

For this specific scenario, SAP own KBA recommends using My Inbox Project Billing Request rather than relying only on My Inbox All Items. SAP also states that the user should have both business catalogs SAP_PS_BC_BILLG_REQ_DISP_PC and SAP_PS_BC_BIL_MYINBOX_PC assigned to the business role

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3491892

Based on that the most defensible conclusion is that the missing bell notification should be treated primarily as a launchpad notification/display issue, not as evidence that Manage Workflow for Project Billing Request is configured incorrectly. The first checks should therefore be the dedicated My Inbox Project Billing Request app and the required catalog assignments. If those are correct and the task is still visible only in Inbox but not in the bell icon this is a valid case to raise with SAP for clarification of notification behavior issue

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Sapphire Expectations& Learnigns
entry.content: People come to SAP Sapphire to explore new tech and build networks, and since I’m attending for the first time, I’m eager to hear what others are most excited about. What breakthroughs are you hoping to see, and how do you expect them to impact the future of enterprise automation and AI? I’d love to hear your thoughts and recommendations for the sessions you're planning to catch!

entry.title: Re: How do I get Object change user activity for Auditing...
entry.content: This is not available in product.

entry.title: Stuck on last step configuring SAP Ariba Invoicing "Schedule Jobs for SAP Ariba Invoicing"
entry.content:
Hello all,

I followed the instructions here: https://help.sap.com/docs/CENTRAL_INVOICE_MANAGEMENT/eed6f62fbe144d7f87d7397d673a2ffe/3c3cddcceab6452b86baf442b762ec40.html to setup and configure an SAP Ariba Invoicing environment. I have it connected to an S4 HANA Public Cloud instance.

I'm stuck at the last mandatory configuration step: "Get the clearing status": https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/ffbdab53bb664cd7857bbb277bab7765.html

The instruction says:

For the first collection of workflow data for a list of specified workflow templates, proceed as follows:
1. Select the Initial Run Indicator
...

From researching online, I'm supposed to be able to see these workflow templates in S4 HANA using the app: "Schedule jobs for CIM". And I have my user configured to see this app in S4 HANA, but it is still not working.

I was wondering if anyone else ran into a similar issue and can help me point to the right direction.

entry.title: Re: HANA does not retain table partitioning when a column...
entry.content: Yes I was aware of that thanks. I was really asking if adding a column to a partitioned table would trigger a merge partitions. I've got an open case.

entry.title: Re: Does Sap datasphere supports transferring wirting out...
entry.content:
No. As of now you cannot push data from DSP to Snowflake directly. Something like Replication Flow from DSP to Snowflake. 

Open Connectors is inbound only. 

Pull from DSP as workaround via either 3rd party (via DB-User) or OData connection. I prefer OData and then just load from DSP to anywhere without the need to expose a DB connection. 

No, DSP is the cloud replacement of the on-premise PI (ex-XI)

SAP Engagement Cloud

entry.title: Re: SAP Sapphire Expectations& Learnigns
entry.content:
Any of these sessions recommend by Peter S are worth your while:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/ai-and-ux-sessions-at-sap-sapphire-2026-that-i-m-most-excited-about/ba-p/14363118

I registered for this preconference: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/orlando/flow/sap/so26/catalog/page/catalog/session/1764953036072001pN6z

The biggest thing, and I always forget, is meeting people.

entry.title: Re: Issue with POSTING_INTERFACE_DOCUMENT, need to select iban
entry.content:
Hi, 

I've tried with BBSEG-IBAN in T_FTPOST and I get a new batch input in sm35. When executing it gives the following error:

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas - Diasble Field
entry.content:
Hi @jan_9979 It may late to answer this Question. Just in case useful for others...

1- Lets not do anything in Personas, do it in RF. Personas will automatically adopt the changes.

2- If its standard FM then you may need to copy and create own custom or you may create a wrapper FM.

3- Once the custom is ready, at location where this field is checked for Required use below set cursor to next field where you need to navigate after skipping GRUND field.

"Make it disabled

CALL METHOD /scwm/cl_rf_bll_srvc=>set_screlm_input_off
EXPORTING
iv_screlm_name = '<GRUND field name>'.

"Make it invisible 

CALL METHOD /scwm/cl_rf_bll_srvc=>set_screlm_invisible_on
EXPORTING
iv_screlm_name = '<GRUND field name>'.

"Set cursor position to next field 

/scwm/cl_rf_bll_srvc=>set_field( < Next field name> ).

 

Hope this helps.

Thanks-

entry.title: Re: Measuring Business Outcomes in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content: @fegutieri  you are 100% correct

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas “P” icon not visible in Fiori Launchp...
entry.content:
Hi @Krishnagokul Hope you have already got the answer but just posting in case other see similar issue.

1- check below roles assign to user or not

/PERSONAS/ADMIN_ROLE
/PERSONAS/EDITOR_ROLE 

2- If Roles assigned then from URL remove below parameter

sap-personas-hidden=X&

Hope this helps...

-Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP for Me – Adoption Report – Drive More Business Value Realization
entry.content: @SalvatorePulvirenti  - The report only provides Public Cloud solutions. So, if a customer is on Private Cloud and is using other Public Cloud solutions (SuccessFactors, etc), they will be able to see them. However, the report is not available for Private Cloud yet.

entry.title: Re: Assignment UUID for Deep Links in SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Thanks for this Ossama,

I am going to try your solution tomorrow. It is disappointing that SAP has the Latest People Profile requiring the use of UUID, without it being out of the box available, instead of the legacy which used user-sys-id.

Hopefully SAP makes the UUID available as I am sure other environment would like to take advantage of the deeplink feature, without creating an object and integration!

Thanks again for your article!

entry.title: First Half 2026 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation?
entry.content:
It’s an exciting time for SAP SuccessFactors customers with our 1H 2026 preview release live as of April 13, 2026.  The production release arrives on May 15, bringing innovations that support how organizations operate through a unified experience. 

The full list of over 400 enhancements can be found in the What’s New Viewer, however, let’s take a look at some of the highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Compensation.

Mass Action Tool Enhancements

The mass action tool within the SAP SuccessFactors Compensation solution now displays supported fields and available corresponding actions.  The refreshed interface aligns bulk edits with established guidelines and pay ranges to help ensure compliance with organizational policies. Planners can improve accuracy by running comparisons to gain insights before applying changes.  This enhancement streamlines compensation planning for administrators by enabling bulk adjustments to custom compensation fields while maintaining visibility into budget impacts.

Mass Action Tool Enhancements

 

 

 

 

 

Custom Formula Enhancements

We've enhanced the basis calculation equation functionality to support additional customization when setting up basis calculation equations for variable pay templates.  Custom formulas now support complex bonus logic, enabling a specific legal entity to configure calculations without requiring multiple pay templates.  This provides greater precision and a more efficient compensation planning experience.

Custom Formula Enhancements

 

 

 

 

 

Automatic Notifications

Additionally, it is now possible to set up email notifications for managers when personal reward statements are generated for their direct reports.  The notifications are sent automatically, simplifying workflows and helping managers stay focused on strategic work.

Learn More

We encourage you to review the resources below to become familiar with all the enhancements and innovations coming with the 1H 2026 release:

  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Brochure
  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Video
  • 1H 2026 Release News Article
  • SAP SuccessFactors Product Release & Road Map Information

entry.title: First Half 2026 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits?
entry.content:
It’s an exciting time for SAP SuccessFactors customers with our 1H 2026 preview release live as of April 13, 2026.  The production release arrives on May 15, bringing innovations that support how organizations operate through a unified experience.  

The full list of over 400 enhancements can be found in the What’s New Viewer, however let’s take a look at some of the highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits.

New Reimbursement Enrollment Experience

We’ve made it easier for employees to enroll in, edit, or opt-out of reimbursement benefits.  Employees can now select their reimbursement using an intuitive design for quick, accurate completion of changes that reduce errors and follow-up inquiries.  The new reimbursement enrollment experience supports all existing benefit types to ensure a smooth transition.

New Reimbursement Enrollment Experience

 

 

 

 

 

Configurable 401(k) Enrollment Experience

We’re pleased to share that US employees can now select their 401(k) plan from within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits.  A clear, configurable experience explains estimated employer contributions and guides them through deferrals and beneficiary setup.  This comes with a robust framework, supporting standard deferral sources, catch-up eligibility, estimated employer contribution calculations, beneficiary setup, and pay component mapping.

Configurable 401(k) Enrollment Experience

 

 

 

 

 

Learn More

We encourage you to review the resources below to become familiar with all the enhancements and innovations coming with the 1H 2026 release:

  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Brochure
  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Video
  • 1H 2026 Release News Article
  • SAP SuccessFactors Product Release & Road Map Information

entry.title: Re: VAT on down-payments
entry.content:
Dear Bohdan,
I have a case where I do a partial clearing of customer down payment with Normal invoice from FB70 
For example, down payment with 10000 including tax 15%=11500 and invoice of 5000 including tax 15% =5750
so, I clear partially In GUI F-32 it's cleared when i do it in Fiori Clear incoming payment (manual clear) system give me error Acct determination for Output tax clearing on down payments with keys not defined in ch/acts 1000 note that i use net tax so no need for tax clearing account appreciated if you can help in this.
My view its system bug
thank you.

entry.title: FBデータを出力する際、プログラムについて
entry.content:
NOTE:3370503にて、DMEEがS/4HANA2029で無効化され、DMEEXに置き換える必要があるとアナウンスされています。

日本では、FBデータを出力する際、プログラム：RFFOJP_T(日本用支払媒体 国内DME)を使用していますが

DMEEXに置き換える必要はない(DMEE廃止による影響はない)と考えて宜しいでしょうか？

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content: @Jack_shueler, take a look at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/odbc/reference/syntax/sqltables-function?view=sql-server-ver17#example

entry.title: SAP FI-CA: API-Based External Collection Agency Integration
entry.content:
1. Introduction

In earlier SAP FI-CA implementations, external collection agency processes were typically implemented using file-based integration, which provided limited visibility and control once receivables were handed over to third-party collection agencies.

API-based integration addresses these limitations, as it enables a structured end-to-end process for managing external collections, improving both operational control and accounting reconciliation within SAP system.

This article provides an implementation view of how receivables are managed in SAP BRIM FI-CA using API-based communication.

2. API-Based Implementation 

2. 1. Receivable Lifecycle in External Collection Process

This section outlines the typical end-to-end process based on real implementation design. 

Figure 1 Receivable Lifecycle in External Collection Agency Process

The process follows these steps.

1. Release items for collection

Overdue receivables are selected based on predefined business rules and assigned to a suitable collection agency.

2. Submission to the external collection agency

Receivables are submitted to the agency via an outbound API, transmitting both debtor and receivable data.

3. Collection activity updates

The agency returns updates via inbound API, including:  

  • Submission confirmations or rejections
  • Status updates and external case references (e.g. court cases)
  • Payment details (principal, interest, charges)
  • Payment reversals
  • Uncollectible receivables

4. Inbound data processing

Inbound data is first stored and then processed in a subsequent step. Processing triggers accounting transactions, including:

  • Posting payment lots
  • Posting interest and charge documents
  • Processing payment reversals via return lots  
  • Writing off uncollectible receivables

5. Posting to collection agency account

Collected amounts are posted as receivables on the agency account.

From an accounting perspective, this is a key step. Once the customer pays the external agency, the customer liability is cleared in the system. However, the company has not yet received the funds. Posting to the agency account ensures this interim position remains visible until the final settlement, which may occur weeks or months later.

6. Settlement from agency

Finally, once funds are received, open items on the agency account are cleared, completing the reconciliation cycle.

2.2 Configuration 

The configuration starts at:

SPRO-> Financial Accounting -> Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable -> Business Transactions -> Submit to Collection Agency

Figure 2 Configuration of External Collection Management

SAP provides flexibility in designing the process, including posting logic, interest and charge handling, and agency determination. These settings are fully driven by business requirements. 

 

API Activation

API communication is activated via:

SPRO-> Financial Accounting -> Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable -> Business Transactions -> Submit to Collection Agency -> Activation of Communication Through API or Enterprise Service

Figure 3 External Collection Agencies – API Activation

If file-based transfers are currently in use, all existing processing must be fully completed before switching to API-based communication in the production environment.

Once activated, file-based integration is no longer supported, and the transaction FP03F (Read Collection Agency File) is disabled and no longer available.

This is especially relevant in FI-CA environments with multiple collection agencies or phased migration strategies, where transition planning becomes a key design activity.

 

Standard APIs

Once activated, SAP uses the following standard interfaces:

  • Outbound: CommissionedOutstandingCollectionsERPBulkRequest_Out
  • Inbound: CommissionedOutstandingCollectionsERPBulkConfirmation_In

 

Functional impact beyond technical change

Shift to API-based communication is not only a technical change. API-based integration also enables additional SAP FI-CA functionality, including improved submission status tracking, better reconciliation with SAP and collection agencies, and more flexibility around grouping of submitted receivables. These capabilities are not available in the file-based approach.

 

Extensibility considerations

In real project scenarios,  out of the box interfaces usually do not fully align with external collection agency requirements.  Data structures and mandatory fields typically require alignment, making extensibility a key part of the technical design.

The main  challenge is not API activation itself, but aligning SAP message structures with the external agency’s interface requirements,  especially around passing mandatory fields.

The first enhancement layer comes from FI-CA events. These enhancement spots allow to extend API messages data:

  • Event 6265 – extend outbound service data
  • Event 6267 – process and map inbound service data

In simple terms, these events allow custom fields to be passed and processed without modifying standard SAP logic.

However, in many implementations, event-based enhancement alone is not sufficient. External agencies usually require additional mandatory fields, which means the interface itself must be extended.

From a clean core perspective, SAP recommends key user extensibility (found via SAP Business Accelerator Hub). Custom fields for outbound interface can be added in the following business contexts:

  • FICA_COLLECTION_AGENCY_BP
  • FICA_COLLECTION_AGENCY_UNIT

However, this capability is currently limited to Public Cloud environments. For Private Cloud deployments, alternative extension approaches must be considered as part of the technical design.

3. Conclusion

The shift from file-based to API-based integration in FI-CA is not just a technical upgrade. It represents a broader improvement in how external collection processes are designed and operated, particularly around automation, real-time visibility, and reconciliation.

When implemented correctly, the solution becomes largely rules-driven, with minimal user involvement beyond exception handling. This makes it well suited for high-volume SAP BRIM environments, where scalability and operational efficiency are key design priorities.

 

Release Version: The functionality described in this article is available in SAP S/4HANA 2025 BRIM FI-CA (Private Cloud). Some capabilities may also be available in earlier releases via SAP Notes (e.g. 3446267).

 

Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not represent official SAP guidance. The examples and approaches are simplified to illustrate possible technical implementation. This is not a complete solution for production use. Do not apply any solution in a production system without fully testing and confirming it in a non-production environment. 

 

Further SAP Help references

Reference:  Submission of Receivables to Collection Agency

entry.title: Re: Issue with release strategy
entry.content:
The transaction CL30N can be used to simulate the release strategy for purchase orders.

If CL30N does not determine the release strategy or displays duplicate strategies, it indicates a problem with the release strategy configuration.

If CL30N determines the correct release strategy but ME21N does not, this could be due to an issue within the user exit EXIT_SAPLEBND_002. The user exit should be deactivated if you do not wish to alter the parameter values for release strategy determination. However, if you prefer to keep the user exit active, ensure that your code includes the line E_CEKKO = I_CEKKO. This line is crucial as it passes the current purchase order data accurately to enable correct release strategy determination.

entry.title: Re: YCOA Account Groups - Verification of Usage and Diffe...
entry.content:
Hello Divyesh,

Thank you very much for your detailed explanation regarding ABST vs RECN! Your response was very helpful and clarified the functional differences perfectly.

I have a follow-up question regarding **Question 1** in my original post - the understanding of each YCOA account group.

Could you please review the table I provided and let me know if there are any **incorrect interpretations**? If so, could you kindly share the **correct definitions** for those account groups?

| Account Group | Text | My Understanding |
|--------------|------|------------------|
| ABST | Recon.account AP/AR | Reconciliation accounts linked to subledgers (AR/AP) |
| ANL. | Fixed assets accounts | Tangible fixed assets (Buildings, Machinery, Land, etc.) |
| ERG. | Income statement accounts | Revenue and expense accounts (Sales Revenue, COGS, SG&A, etc.) |
| FIN. | Liquid funds accounts | Cash and highly liquid assets (Petty Cash, Bank Accounts, etc.) |
| MAT. | Materials management accounts | Inventory-related accounts (Raw Materials, WIP, Finished Goods, etc.) |
| RECN | ReconAcct ARAP Ready for Input | Reconciliation accounts allowing direct manual posting for AR/AP |
| SAKO | G/L accounts (general) | General GL accounts (Short-term Investments, Notes Payable, etc.) |
| SASL | G/L accounts (ledger-spec. OI) | Ledger-specific accounts |
| SECC | Secondary costs / revenues | Secondary cost elements for cost allocation |

I want to ensure my documentation is accurate for our project.

Thank you again for your support!

Best regards,
byd

I have a follow-up question regarding **Question 1** in my original post - the understanding of each YCOA account group. Could you please review the table I provided and let me know if there are any **incorrect interpretations**? If so, could you kindly share the **correct definitions** for those account groups? | Account Group | Text | My Understanding | |--------------|------|------------------| | ABST | Recon.account AP/AR | Reconciliation accounts linked to subledgers (AR/AP) | | ANL. | Fixed assets accounts | Tangible fixed assets (Buildings, Machinery, Land, etc.) | | ERG. | Income statement accounts | Revenue and expense accounts (Sales Revenue, COGS, SG&A, etc.) | | FIN. | Liquid funds accounts | Cash and highly liquid assets (Petty Cash, Bank Accounts, etc.) | | MAT. | Materials management accounts | Inventory-related accounts (Raw Materials, WIP, Finished Goods, etc.) | | RECN | ReconAcct ARAP Ready for Input | Reconciliation accounts allowing direct manual posting for AR/AP | | SAKO | G/L accounts (general) | General GL accounts (Short-term Investments, Notes Payable, etc.) | | SASL | G/L accounts (ledger-spec. OI) | Ledger-specific accounts | | SECC | Secondary costs / revenues | Secondary cost elements for cost allocation | I want to ensure my documentation is accurate for our project. Thank you again for your support! Best regards, byd

entry.title: Re: Seamlessly Transitioning Your Digital Learning to WalkMe Learning Arc
entry.content:

Hosting all our training materials and personal information offshore is a no-go. 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content: The referenced web page has a correct example. It uses a 1-byte SQL_C_CHAR (SQLCHAR type). You modified the datatype to 2-byte SQL_C_WCHAR but did not double the buffer length.

entry.title: Re: AI for SAP TRM: Generate your BRFplus Application from Business Rules in Natural Language
entry.content: Great stuff! Is this wizard only available in TRM or will it also become available in other modules than TRM, like PSCD and FICA that have various processes that make use of BRF+?

entry.title: SAP EWM Basic vs Advanced: Risk based Assessment Criteria for CPG Warehousing Scenarios
entry.content:
Summary

When organisations assess SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the evaluation often starts with a simple question: “Does Basic EWM fulfil our functional requirements?”

While this is a valid starting point, experience from multiple SAP S/4HANA engagements shows that functional coverage alone is not a sufficient decision criterion. In Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) environments in particular, warehouse operations tend to evolve rapidly due to seasonality, promotion‑driven peaks, SKU proliferation, and service‑level pressure.

This blog provides a risk‑based assessment framework to help architects and programme leaders decide when Advanced EWM should be considered, even if Basic EWM appears sufficient today.

It complements the SAP Community article “SAP EWM Deployment Options – Embedded vs De-central Warehouse Management” by focusing specifically on capability scope within Embedded EWM rather than deployment architecture. Reference: SAP EWM Deployment Options – Embedded vs Decentral Warehouse Management

 

Background

SAP S/4HANA offers two strategic deployment models for Extended Warehouse Management:

  • Embedded EWM, running within the S/4HANA system (Basic or Advanced)
  • Decentralised EWM, running on a separate S/4HANA instance

The choice between Embedded and Decentralised EWM is primarily an enterprise architecture decision, driven by factors such as:

  • Multi‑ERP integration
  • Performance isolation
  • Automation intensity
  • Independent upgrade cycles

These considerations are explained in detail in the SAP Community Enterprise Architecture Knowledge Base article referenced above. However, within Embedded EWM, customers must make an additional decision that is often underestimated:

Should Embedded EWM be implemented with a Basic or an Advanced licence?

Because Basic EWM is included with SAP S/4HANA, many programmes default to it without fully assessing future warehouse risk and evolution, particularly in industries such as CPG where operational volatility is the norm rather than the exception.

 

Assessing Basic vs Advanced EWM: Beyond Functional Fit

Reframing the Decision: Execution vs Optimisation

The distinction between Basic and Advanced EWM is not only about which features are technically available at go‑live.

  • Basic EWM supports stable warehouse execution for predictable processes and limited optimisation needs.
  • Advanced EWM supports continuous optimisation, higher throughput, and evolving business models.

In CPG warehouses, requirements rarely remain static. A site that starts with straightforward pallet handling may later need to support:

  • Promotion‑driven surge volumes
  • Mixed‑case picking
  • Faster outbound cut‑off times
  • Increased customer segmentation

The key assessment question therefore becomes: Is the warehouse expected to remain execution‑focused, or will optimisation and adaptability become critical?

Key Assessment Criteria

Below are common conditions under which Advanced EWM should be evaluated, even when Basic EWM meets current scope.

Business Criticality

In CPG supply chains, regional and central distribution centres often sit directly on the critical path to revenue.
Missed despatch windows during promotions or peak seasons can have immediate commercial impact.

Advanced EWM reduces operational risk in warehouses where service levels directly affect retail availability.

Demand Volatility and Seasonality

CPG warehouses typically face:

  • Strong seasonality (e.g. summer beverages, festive demand)
  • Sudden demand spikes driven by promotions
  • Rapid SKU introductions and retirements

Advanced EWM offers greater flexibility to manage volume‑driven variability without redesigning core processes later.

Shift from FIFO Execution to Throughput Optimisation

Many CPG sites start with basic FIFO picking but later require:

  • Prioritised outbound waves
  • Dynamic work assignment for peak periods
  • Improved visibility into warehouse workload

These optimisation patterns are inherently better supported withAdvanced EWM.

Automation as a Medium‑Term Option

Even if automation is not part of the initial scope, CPG organisations frequently evaluate:

  • Conveyor integration
  • Automated pallet handling
  • High‑speed picking areas for fast movers

As highlighted in SAP architecture guidance, highly automated or high‑throughput warehouses typically require advanced orchestration and control capabilities, particularly as complexity increases.

Licensing and Compliance Considerations

Certain EWM design patterns can implicitly activate advanced functionality, creating potential licence compliance risk if only Basic EWM is contractually covered.

From a governance perspective, selecting Advanced EWM upfront removes ambiguity, particularly in large multi‑site CPG programmes.

 

Risk‑Based Decision Model

Rather than relying solely on feature checklists, we may want to apply a risk‑based assessment per warehouse.

Typical assessment questions: Score each site YES / NO

  • Will volumes / SKUs / Channels / picking-complexity increase?
  • Is peak performance business‑critical?
  • Are promotions or seasonal surges difficult to absorb today?
  • Is future automation plausible?
  • Is audit / licence risk unacceptable?
  • Would architectural flexibility be required later? e.g. Might need to be decentralised later?

Guideline outcome:

  • Low risk (0–1 YES) → Basic EWM may be sufficient
  • Medium risk (2–3 YES) → Advanced EWM strongly recommended
  • High risk (4+ YES) → Advanced EWM effectively mandatory

This approach aligns well with CPG environments where change is expected rather than exceptional.

 

Conclusion

In CPG supply chains, warehouse requirements rarely remain unchanged after go‑live. Promotions, seasonality, customer demands, and network optimisation continuously increase operational pressure.

While Basic EWM can be a valid tactical choice for stable, low‑risk warehouses, Advanced EWM provides strategic resilience where growth, volatility, or optimisation are expected.

When used together with the architectural guidance in the SAP Community article on Embedded vs Decentral EWM deployment, organisations can make a coherent, forward‑looking decision that avoids costly redesigns later.

Ultimately, the most successful EWM programmes are those that treat capability choice as a business risk decision, not just a functional one.

Typical Decision Guidance

  • Choose EWM Basic if:
    • Manual operations dominate
    • SKU velocity is low
    • Process orchestration requirements are limited
    • Cost sensitivity is high
  • Choose EWM Advanced if:
    • There are high throughput and/or significant peak volumes
    • Automation or robotics is planned or already in place
    • Labour productivity optimisation is a key objective
    • Complex outbound orchestration is required (e.g. waves, cross-docking, value-added services)
    • There is an opportunity to reduce the existing WM custom code base rather than migrate it
      • For example, if bespoke developments (such as wave management solutions) exist across a subset of warehouses, adopting EWM Advanced for those locations can enable a return to standard functionality
      • This avoids carrying forward custom developments into S/4, thereby supporting a clean core approach and reducing both migration effort and long-term maintenance overhead
    • There is an intention to leverage advanced and emerging capabilities, including any current or future AI-driven capabilities and AI Agents

entry.title: The Next Chapter in Our AI Journey: From Machine Learning to Agentic AI
entry.content:
A few years ago, I started a series documenting our collective Journey to Machine Learning with SAP S/4HANA. At that time, we were heavily focused on embedding predictive analytics into our core ERP processes—using algorithms to forecast demand, detect anomalies, and automate highly structured, repetitive tasks.

Since then, the landscape has evolved at a blistering pace. Generative AI arrived and gave our systems the ability to understand natural language and create content. But as Enterprise Architects, we quickly realized that while copilots and chatbots are incredibly helpful, they are still fundamentally passive. They wait for our instructions.

Today, we are standing at the threshold of a much more profound architectural shift: Agentic AI.

We are officially moving from Automation to Autonomy.

The Paradigm Shift: What is Agentic AI?

Traditional AI has historically helped by answering questions or triggering isolated, hard-coded workflows. Agentic AI represents a shift where AI moves from merely supporting work to taking ownership of entire segments of it.

Instead of waiting for a human to connect the dots across different systems, Agentic AI introduces "agency." These AI agents are designed to understand a complex business intent, assess the enterprise context, formulate a multi-step plan, and execute tasks autonomously across SAP and non-SAP landscapes.

Think of them less as software features, and more as trusted digital coworkers.

The Architectural Puzzle (A Peek Under the Hood)

Conceptually, having a "digital coworker" sounds amazing. But for Enterprise Architects, it immediately raises massive, complex questions:

  • Context: How do you ensure an LLM doesn't hallucinate a financial accrual?

  • Memory: How do you give an AI the ability to track a long-running, multi-day procurement cycle without losing its place?

  • Execution: How do you give an AI "Hands" to securely execute a BAPI or update a record in SAP SuccessFactors without breaking compliance?

  • Orchestration: How do you coordinate a "swarm" of specialized agents—say, a Supply Chain agent and a Finance agent—to collaborate on a sudden market disruption?

These are the exact enterprise design challenges my colleague and co-author, Sonal Gupta, and I set out to solve. Sonal’s deep expertise in enterprise architecture and advisory has been absolutely instrumental in shaping how we transition these autonomous concepts from theoretical models into governed, scalable, and secure realities on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

But we knew we couldn't answer these questions in a vacuum. To build a truly robust blueprint, we needed to tap into the collective intelligence of our industry. Over the past several months, Sonal and I engaged in extensive research and dialogue—pressure-testing these concepts with fellow Enterprise Architects and synthesizing insights from recent AI industry events. By actively participating in vibrant AI communities and looking holistically across the entire AI ecosystem—drawing from both deep SAP expertise and the broader non-SAP technology landscape—we were able to distill what is actually working in the field.

Coming This Week (#17 week): The Full Blueprint

Next week, we will be officially releasing the culmination of this research in our joint whitepaper: "From Automation to Autonomy: The Era of Agentic AI in the Intelligent Enterprise".

If you are an IT leader, developer, or Enterprise Architect trying to figure out how to safely scale AI beyond simple chat interfaces, this whitepaper is your blueprint.

Here is a sneak peek of what we will unpack:

  • The 5 Pillars of an SAP Agent: The technical constitution required to build agents with true reasoning, memory, and execution capabilities.

  • Joule as the Cognitive Router: How to utilize SAP Joule and the Agent2Agent (A2A) framework to break down historic enterprise silos.

  • Economic & Risk Governance: Hard-hitting strategies on how to prevent "LLM API taxation" and build blast-radius containment to catch AI hallucinations before they impact the business.

  • The 7-Step Implementation Roadmap: A practical guide to moving your organization from isolated AI pilots to an enterprise-wide digital workforce.

I invite you to stay tuned, read the upcoming whitepaper next week, and join Sonal and me in this conversation. The transition from Machine Learning to Agentic AI isn’t just an IT upgrade—it’s a fundamental reimagining of how the Intelligent Enterprise operates.

Let me know in the comments: What is your biggest concern or most anticipated use case when it comes to autonomous AI agents in your SAP landscape?

entry.title: change system id on an existing case
entry.content:
change system id on an existing case

Question and Answer:
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: General Customizing Related to IDoc processing
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Logical Systems
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Define Logical System



Manually selected component: XX-SER-FORME
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: change system id on an existing case
entry.content:
As discussing, this issue is explained in KBA 2543254 - Incorrect System ID in a case - SAP for Me it is not possible to change the system (or System ID) on an existing case.

That means you have two option, provide the correct system detail in the existing case or create a new case with correct system.

entry.content:
@KevinR , @noravonthenen

entry.title: Re: want to generate trial balance based on business place in public cloud
entry.content: We’ve marked the answer as the accepted solution to assist you. If you have more questions, you can un-accept it whenever needed.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Account Determination of OBYC - GBB (VAX/VAY) and WRX
entry.content: Removed general tag.

entry.title: Re: Does PRIMARY KEY clustered check stored somewhere
entry.content: @chris_keating, I came up with the following query - SELECT st.table_name, si.index_name, st.clustered_index_id, idx.indextype FROM sys.SYSTAB st, sys.SYSIDX si, sys.SYSINDEXES idx WHERE st.table_id = si.table_id AND st.table_name = 'leagues' AND idx.iname = si.index_name AND idx.indextype = 'primary key';. But I think my where can be improved...

entry.title: Re: Native Promotion Engine Crashes Due to Integer Overflow When numberOfUnits Exceeds Integer.MAX_V
entry.content:
Hello Fadel,

Could you help confirm the below information for us to validate further

1. Version of Commerce Cloud in which the mentioned issue is seen - whether it is JDK17 or JDK21 ?

2. Is this a new implementation that you are trying to test this or was it working before ?

3. Additionally have you raised any support ticket for this issue ?

 

Kind Regards

Balaji

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hi Kerisha,

Thanks for the detailed explanation!

We I did the restricted configuration for "Read, Value Help" and "Value Help" as below,

I only set authorization group A for material A2C7860730699, but I still can check the sales order with other parts number. Pls check the below example.

I also try to maintain authorization group for product for "Write, Read, Value Help", "Read, Value Help" and "Value Help"

But I met the following error when I checked the sales order, even check the material A2C7860730699 (order70000068) which has maintained the authorization group.

Could you provide some suggestions for this case? Thanks!

Best regards,

Joey

entry.title: Re: Why has cut-and-paste behaviour changed? [Rant]
entry.content: Hi @Robotperson, as this is a new feature related concern, please try to reach out the product team via raising a ticket under the WZ component https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/getting-support, or you could raise feature request via influence portal https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279

entry.title: Re: Settings for the automatic creation of cProject from a PPM Item followed by creation of PS Proje
entry.content:
@Gopal123 : For sending the Planned cost to PM and rolling back of Actual cost please refer my another Article at https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blog-posts-by-members/settings-for-budget-planned-cost-transfer-to-ps-project-and-actual-cost/ba-p/14273304

Regards,

Krishna Singh

entry.title: Re: Settings for the automatic creation of cProject from a PPM Item followed by creation of PS Proje
entry.content:
@Fox_Chen : As per my understanding, if you are dealing only with PPM Item and cProject then Yes, you have to map Project Template with Item Type, could you please explain the issue in more detail? Where exactly system is asking for Item template for automatic creation of cProject?

Regards,

Krishna Singh
